learning_outcomes.description: Analyse the complex and interrelated sustainability challenges facing organisations.
Evaluate climate policy developments and their strategic implications for business.
Collaboratively design innovative strategic responses to sustainability challenges.
Propose strategies for organisational adaptation and transformation.
Reflect on personal learning, team dynamics, and leadership capacity to influence sustainability outcomes in organisational contexts.

assessments.task_description:
Responding to sustainability issues requires companies to understand the complexity and wicked problem climate change represents. This assessment is an individual reflection based on participation in the in-class (week 5) climate negotiation simulation. The reflection consists of a single long-form (1,500 words) essay. This assessment helps students develop systems thinking and evaluative insights. Details of the assessment questions are provided in Blackboard.

This is an individual piece of assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments are required to be submitted electronically through Turnitin in the Blackboard Assessment link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment is a group project involving the creation of a strategic scenario plan. The assessment emphasises collaboration and forward-thinking strategy. Detailed instructions will be given through in-class workshops (Weeks 7-9). Students will form teams in class. Teams will be working on scenarios developed during class workshops. Each team will choose a key sustainability issue to focus on - as a team you will need to research the issue and develop a strategic management response in line with the scenarios developed in class. These plans must be plausible.

For this assessment student teams are required to submit:

A video presentation of their scenario and a report with supporting documentation must be submitted online through the Blackboard site. Please note the video presentation should stand alone to convey all content with the supplementary material for reference purposes.

Detailed marking criteria for this Assessment will be provided on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This 1000 word individual assignment is a critical reflection on the scenario planning activity and its role as a management tool to prepare for future challenges. Further details will be provided in the course Blackboard folder.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This assessment is an individual oral assessment/interview focused on personal insights, learning journey, and course concept application. It assesses professional communication and reflective capacity. The assessment interview will be around 10 minutes and focus on 3 overarching questions. More information around the assessment will be provided in-class in week 13.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
